component.name: EasyAmp
component.summary: Classic-player-style media player with a 10-band EQ

description.p: EasyAmp is a self-contained classic-player-style media player — a loving tribute to the late-90s desktop audio player, built fresh in GTK4 with original artwork.

ul.li: Plays local music (MP3, FLAC, WAV, OGG, Opus, M4A and more)
Built-in 10-band graphic EQ with a response curve and presets
Live spectrum analyzer and analog VU meters

description.p: Maintenance release — verifies the cross-platform update badge and self-update flow end to end.
The "update available" badge now appears on every platform (including the Flatpak) and links to the update page.
Add LED-style left/right level meters to the equalizer view, and tidy the control panel into a row of knobs and a row of buttons.
Stability fix: resolves a freeze that could occur when changing tracks. (The per-channel L/R split was removed for now — it deadlocked the audio pipeline; it'll return in a more robust form.)

Usability fixes: double-click a playlist track to play (single click just selects), a failed track now stops with a clear message instead of skipping through the list, a fluid ~4:3 window, and the equalizer controls laid out in two tidy rows.
The pitch (varispeed) control now works on macOS and the Flatpak as well, via the built-in resampler.
Add independent left/right EQ: a SPLIT toggle in the equalizer view lets you tune each channel separately (linked stereo by default).

Major equalizer update: a full-window parametric EQ (10–32 bands with per-band frequency/Q), reachable from a new Player/Equalizer tab, with knob controls for gains, pitch and balance, a spectrum + waveform display, and Equalizer-APO / GraphicEQ import & export.
Add a status footer that shows the running version and flags when a newer build is available (Windows/macOS).
Fix a grey overlay on the Presets button under the Flatpak theme.
Native Windows build (installer + portable), with WASAPI-loopback visualizer capture. Cross-platform fixes: Cairo-drawn transport icons and more robust stylesheet loading.
Build numpy from source in the Flatpak (per Flathub review); macOS .app/.dmg available.
Fix button rendering in the Flatpak; build numpy for both x86_64 and aarch64.
Self-contained player + 10-band EQ with Bass/Loudness toggles, spectrum and analog VU visualizers, and Flatpak packaging.
